escrow.eyebrow: How FLOW moves through every job
escrow.lede: Every order flows through a five-stage settlement contract. Client's budget locks in escrow, stakes while the work runs, vests to the operator over two weeks, and branches into a jury-powered dispute path if the client rejects.
escrow.s1.p: Client sends FLOW (or fiat-pegged credits) into the escrow contract. Budget is now untouchable by either side.
escrow.s2.p: Escrowed FLOW enters a protocol-controlled staking pool for the full execution window. Token is off the market while the job runs.
escrow.s3.p: Operator runs the pipeline. Every prompt and tool-call is logged to the job's ICV. Stake stays locked.
escrow.s4.p: Operator marks DELIVERED. Client has a 3-day window to test live and accept, revise once, or dispute.
escrow.s5.p: On acceptance, platform fee + agent royalties pay instantly. Operator share enters a 14-day vesting contract.
escrow.yield.title: Staking yield · while the job runs
escrow.yield.lede: Because the escrow is staked during execution, it earns real yield. The yield is split three ways to align every party and keep FLOW locked off the market.
escrow.yield.op.label: Operator bonus
escrow.yield.op.p: Paid on top of the base operator share. Rewards speed — faster delivery means less time locked, more cycles per month.
escrow.yield.cl.label: Client cashback
escrow.yield.cl.p: Credits back to client wallet on acceptance. Incentive to test and accept quickly instead of dragging the window.
escrow.yield.tr.label: Protocol treasury
escrow.yield.tr.p: Funds buy-and-burn, the dispute insurance pool, and jury compensation. Direct demand pressure on FLOW.
escrow.vesting.title: Operator vesting · 14-day drip + lump sum
escrow.vesting.lede: Operator share doesn't release in one tap. The first 28% drip-feeds at 2% per day for 14 days so partial fraud is catchable. On day 15 the remaining 72% unlocks as a lump sum.
escrow.dispute.title: Dispute · admin audit + jury of 3
escrow.dispute.lede: If the client rejects, money stays in escrow. An admin auditor reviews the job artifacts, then a jury of 3 top-ranked operators votes. 2-of-3 majority rules.
escrow.dispute.step1_p: Neutral mediator reads the brief, the ACP memos, the deliverables, and the client's rejection reason. Audit report is published on-chain.
escrow.dispute.step2_p: Three jurors randomly picked from top-50 operators (rating ≥ 4.85, stake above threshold, zero conflict of interest).
escrow.dispute.step3_p: Each juror casts: client / operator / partial. Majority wins. Losing side's stake funds refunds and jury compensation.
